Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: User is not already registered in the system.
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., test@example.com), valid password (e.g., Passw0rd!), valid name (e.g., John Doe)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters valid email, password, and name.
3. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result: System confirms registration success and redirects to login page.

Title: Registration with Invalid Email Format
Preconditions: User is not registered.
Test Data: Invalid email (e.g., test@example), valid password, valid name
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the registration page.
2. User enters invalid email, valid password, and name.
3. User clicks the "Register" button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating invalid email format.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: User is registered in the system.
Test Data: Valid registered email (e.g., test@example.com), correct password (e.g., Passw0rd!)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters valid email and password.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: System grants access to the user dashboard.

Title: Login with Incorrect Password
Preconditions: User is registered.
Test Data: Valid registered email, incorrect password (e.g., wrongpass)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the login page.
2. User enters valid email and incorrect password.
3. User clicks the "Login" button.
Expected Result: System displays an error message indicating incorrect credentials.

Title: Display Weather Data
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the weather visualization section.
2. User requests weather data display.
Expected Result: System displays current weather data (e.g., temperature, humidity, etc.).

Title: View List of Greenhouses
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse registered.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the greenhouse list section.
2. User requests to view all greenhouses.
Expected Result: System displays a list of all registered greenhouses with their details.

Title: Add New Greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Valid greenhouse name (e.g., Greenhouse A), valid location (e.g., Garden), valid sensor data (e.g., temperature sensor)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the greenhouse addition section.
2. User enters greenhouse name, location, and sensor details.
3. User clicks the "Add Greenhouse" button.
Expected Result: System confirms greenhouse addition and updates the greenhouse list.

Title: Remove Existing Greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse registered.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the greenhouse list section.
2. User selects a greenhouse to remove.
3. User confirms removal.
Expected Result: System removes the greenhouse and updates the list accordingly.

Title: Modify User Settings
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: New language preference (e.g., English), new notification setting (e.g., Enable)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the settings section.
2. User modifies language preference and notification settings.
3. User saves changes.
Expected Result: System applies the changes and confirms updates.

Title: View Latest Data for Greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse with recorded data.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the latest data section.
2. User selects a greenhouse.
Expected Result: System displays the latest recorded data for the selected greenhouse.

Title: View Data Graphs for Greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse with recorded data.
Test Data: Existing greenhouse name (e.g., Greenhouse A), time range (e.g., Last 7 days)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the graphs section.
2. User selects a greenhouse and a time range.
Expected Result: System displays a graph of the selected data for the chosen time range.

Title: Manage Connected Hardware
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one connected hardware device.
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the hardware management section.
2. User selects a device to manage (e.g., view status, update firmware).
Expected Result: System displays device details and allows management actions (e.g., status update, firmware update confirmation).

Title: Update User Profile Information
Preconditions: User is logged in.
Test Data: Updated profile details (e.g., new phone number, new address)
Test Steps:
1. User navigates to the profile section.
2. User updates personal information (e.g., phone number, address).
3. User saves changes.
Expected Result: System confirms profile update and reflects changes in the user profile.

Title: Successful User Registration
Preconditions: App installed on device, device connected to internet
Test Data: Valid name, valid email, valid password
Test Steps:
1. Open the registration screen.
2. Enter a valid name in the name field.
3. Enter a valid email in the email field.
4. Enter a valid password in the password field.
5. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is created successfully, and a confirmation message is displayed.

Title: Registration Error for Existing User
Preconditions: App installed on device, device connected to internet, an existing user with the same email
Test Data: Name, email of an existing user, valid password
Test Steps:
1. Open the registration screen.
2. Enter a name in the name field.
3. Enter an email of an existing user in the email field.
4. Enter a valid password in the password field.
5. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed stating the user is already registered.

Title: Successful User Login
Preconditions: App installed on device, device connected to internet, registered user
Test Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter a valid email in the email field.
3. Enter a valid password in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is logged in successfully and redirected to the user profile/dashboard.

Title: Login Error for Unregistered User
Preconditions: App installed on device, device connected to internet
Test Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter a non-registered email in the email field.
3. Enter a valid password in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed stating the user is not registered.

Title: Login Error for Incorrect Credentials
Preconditions: App installed on device, device connected to internet, registered user
Test Steps:
1. Open the login screen.
2. Enter a valid email in the email field.
3. Enter an incorrect password in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed stating the credentials are incorrect.

Title: Error When Adding Already Associated Greenhouse
Preconditions: User logged in, device connected to internet, greenhouse already associated with another user
Test Data: Greenhouse already associated with another user
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse addition screen.
2. Attempt to select the greenhouse already associated with another user.
3. Confirm the association.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed stating the greenhouse is already associated with another user.

Title: Error When Adding Non-Existent Greenhouse
Preconditions: User logged in, device connected to internet
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse addition screen.
2. Attempt to select a non-existent greenhouse.
3. Confirm the association.
Expected Result: Error message is displayed stating the greenhouse does not exist.

Title: Modify greenhouse temperature limits within valid range
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the temperature settings option.
3. Enter min=15 and max=30 for temperature limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Settings are saved successfully. No error messages appear.

Title: Modify greenhouse humidity limits within valid range
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the humidity settings option.
3. Enter min=40 and max=70 for air humidity limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Settings are saved successfully. No error messages appear.

Title: Modify greenhouse soil humidity limits within valid range
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the soil humidity settings option.
3. Enter min=30 and max=60 for soil humidity limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Settings are saved successfully. No error messages appear.

Title: Modify greenhouse light limits within valid range
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the light settings option.
3. Enter min=500 and max=1500 for light limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Settings are saved successfully. No error messages appear.

Title: Modify greenhouse name
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the greenhouse name option.
3. Enter "Greenhouse A" as the new name.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Greenhouse name is updated successfully. No error messages appear.

Title: Set temperature limits below zero (invalid)
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the temperature settings option.
3. Enter min=-5 and max=20 for temperature limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Error message appears: "Valori limite inferiori a 0." Settings are not saved.

Title: Set temperature limits above 100 (invalid)
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the temperature settings option.
3. Enter min=50 and max=120 for temperature limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Error message appears: "Valori limite maggiori di 100." Settings are not saved.

Title: Set min value higher than max value (invalid)
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the temperature settings option.
3. Enter min=30 and max=20 for temperature limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Error message appears: "Valori di minimo superiori ai valori di massimo." Settings are not saved.

Title: Set max value lower than min value (invalid)
Preconditions: User has logged in. Device is connected to the internet. Greenhouse is linked to the user account.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings section.
2. Select the temperature settings option.
3. Enter min=10 and max=5 for temperature limits.
4. Click "Save" or "Apply."
Expected Result: Error message appears: "Valori di massimo inferiori ai valori di minimo." Settings are not saved.

Title: Successful User Registration with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the application.
Test Steps:
1. Enter "Test User" in the name field.
2. Enter "testuser@example.com" in the email field.
3. Enter "SecurePass123" in the password field.
4. Confirm the password by re-entering "SecurePass123".
5. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: User account is successfully created, and a confirmation message appears. User is redirected to the login page.

Title: User Registration with Invalid Email Format
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the application.
Test Steps:
1. Enter "Test User" in the name field.
2. Enter "invalidemail" in the email field.
3. Enter "SecurePass123" in the password field.
4. Confirm the password by re-entering "SecurePass123".
5. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: Error message appears stating "Invalid email format." User remains on the registration page.

Title: User Registration with Weak Password
Preconditions: User is on the registration page of the application.
Test Steps:
1. Enter "Test User" in the name field.
2. Enter "testuser@example.com" in the email field.
3. Enter "weak" in the password field.
4. Confirm the password by re-entering "weak".
5. Click the "Register" button.
Expected Result: Error message appears stating "Password must meet complexity requirements." User remains on the registration page.

Title: Successful User Login with Valid Credentials
Preconditions: User is on the login page of the application. A valid user account exists.
Test Steps:
1. Enter "testuser@example.com" in the email field.
2. Enter "SecurePass123" in the password field.
3. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: User is successfully logged in and redirected to the dashboard or home page.

Title: Failed User Login with Invalid Password
Preconditions: User is on the login page of the application. A valid user account exists.
Test Steps:
1. Enter "testuser@example.com" in the email field.
2. Enter "WrongPass123" in the password field.
3. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: Error message appears stating "Invalid email or password." User remains on the login page.

Title: Insert a new greenhouse for a user
Preconditions: User is logged in and has no greenhouses assigned
Test Data: Valid greenhouse name, ID, and user credentials
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse insertion section.
2. Enter a valid greenhouse name and ID.
3. Submit the form.
Expected Result: The greenhouse is successfully added to the user’s account and marked as occupied.

Title: Insert multiple greenhouses for a user
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse assigned
Test Data: Valid greenhouse names and IDs (e.g., "Greenhouse A," "Greenhouse B")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse insertion section.
2. Enter the first greenhouse name and ID, then submit.
3. Verify the first greenhouse is added.
4. Enter a second greenhouse name and ID, then submit.
Expected Result: Both greenhouses are successfully added to the user’s account and visible in the list.

Title: Remove an unused greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse assigned
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse list.
2. Select the greenhouse to remove.
3. Confirm the removal action.
Expected Result: The selected greenhouse is removed from the user’s account and no longer appears in the list.

Title: Modify alert thresholds for a greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse assigned
Test Data: Valid max/min threshold values (e.g., temperature: max 35°C, min 10°C)
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings for a specific greenhouse.
2. Enter new max/min threshold values for alerts.
3. Save the changes.
Expected Result: The new threshold values are applied, and future alerts will be triggered based on these limits.

Title: Rename a greenhouse for better visibility
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse assigned
Test Data: New greenhouse name (e.g., "Greenhouse North" → "Main Production Greenhouse")
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse settings for a specific greenhouse.
2. Enter a new name for the greenhouse.
3. Save the changes.
Expected Result: The greenhouse name is updated in the system and displayed correctly in all relevant sections.

Title: View the latest collected data from a greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in, has at least one greenhouse assigned, and the greenhouse has collected data
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse data section.
2. Select the greenhouse to view.
3. Check the "latest data" tab.
Expected Result: The most recent data (e.g., temperature, humidity) collected from the greenhouse is displayed.

Title: View historical greenhouse data in graphical format
Preconditions: User is logged in, has at least one greenhouse assigned, and the greenhouse has historical data
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the greenhouse data section.
2. Select the greenhouse to view.
3. Check the "historical data" or "grafici" (graphs) tab.
Expected Result: A graphical representation (e.g., line chart, bar graph) of the historical data (e.g., temperature trends over time) is displayed.

Title: Activate irrigation system for a greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in, has at least one greenhouse assigned, and the greenhouse has an active irrigation system
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the hardware control section for the greenhouse.
2. Select the irrigation system option.
3. Activate the irrigation system.
Expected Result: The irrigation system is turned on, and the system confirms activation (e.g., status update or visual indicator).

Title: Deactivate lighting system for a greenhouse
Preconditions: User is logged in, has at least one greenhouse assigned, and the greenhouse has an active lighting system
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the hardware control section for the greenhouse.
2. Select the lighting system option.
3. Deactivate the lighting system.
Expected Result: The lighting system is turned off, and the system confirms deactivation (e.g., status update or visual indicator).

Title: Modify User Profile Data
Preconditions: User is logged in and navigates to the profile modification section
Test Data: Valid email (e.g., "user@example.com"), new password (e.g., "NewPass123!"), existing username
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the "Modifica profilo" (Edit Profile) option.
2. User enters a new email address in the email field.
3. User enters a new password in the password field.
4. User confirms changes by clicking "Salva" (Save).
Expected Result: The system updates the user’s email and password without errors, and the changes are reflected in the profile section.

Title: Verify Android Version Compatibility
Preconditions: None
Test Data: Android 8.1 emulator/device, Android 9 emulator/device, Android 10 emulator/device
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android 8.1 device/emulator.
2. Launch the application and verify all primary features (e.g., login, profile access) work without errors.
3. Repeat steps 1-2 on Android 9 and Android 10 devices/emulators.
Expected Result: The application launches and functions correctly on all tested Android versions (8.1, 9, 10) without crashes or compatibility issues.

Title: Verify Application Response Time
Preconditions: Application is installed and opened on a device with stable internet
Test Steps:
1. Measure the time taken to load the main dashboard after launching the app.
2. Measure the time taken to load user profile data after clicking "Modifica profilo."
3. Measure the time taken to load statistics for a selected greenhouse.
Expected Result: All operations complete within a reasonable time frame (e.g., <3 seconds for dashboard, <2 seconds for profile, <4 seconds for statistics) without freezing or lag.

Title: Prevent Crashes During Database Communication
Preconditions: Application is connected to a live database with active sensors
Test Data: Simulated network interruption, invalid sensor data, database timeout
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a temporary network interruption while the app is fetching data from the database.
2. Simulate invalid sensor data being sent to the app.
3. Force a database timeout during a data retrieval operation.
Expected Result: The application handles all simulated errors gracefully (e.g., displays a user-friendly error message, retries connection, or falls back to cached data) without crashing.

Title: Add and Remove Multiple Greenhouses
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one greenhouse configured
Test Steps:
1. User clicks on the option to add a new greenhouse.
2. User enters "Serra1" as the name and saves.
3. User repeats step 2 to add "Serra2" and "Serra3."
4. User selects "Serra2" and clicks the option to remove it.
Expected Result: The system adds all three greenhouses successfully and removes "Serra2" without errors, updating the list accordingly.

Title: Verify Clean and Intuitive UI
Preconditions: Application is launched on a device
Test Steps:
1. Navigate through the main dashboard, profile section, and greenhouse management screens.
2. Verify that all buttons, icons, and menus are clearly labeled and easily identifiable.
3. Check that the layout is consistent across all screens (e.g., no cluttered elements, logical grouping of features).
Expected Result: The UI is visually clean, intuitive, and consistent, with no ambiguous labels or confusing layouts.

Title: Display Background Statistics for Selected Greenhouse
Preconditions: User has at least one greenhouse configured and is on the greenhouse selection screen
Test Data: A greenhouse with recorded statistics (e.g., temperature, humidity)
Test Steps:
1. User selects a greenhouse from the list.
2. User minimizes the app to background (e.g., by pressing the home button).
3. User reopens the app and checks if the last recorded statistics for the selected greenhouse are visible.
Expected Result: The app displays the latest statistics for the selected greenhouse even when reopened from the background.

Title: Verify application displays in multiple languages
Preconditions: Application installed on a device with supported languages configured
Test Data: English, Italian, Spanish (or any two languages explicitly supported)
Test Steps:
1. Open the application on a device configured to display in English.
2. Navigate to the language selection menu (if available) or change device language to Italian.
3. Restart the application.
4. Verify all UI elements (buttons, labels, error messages) are displayed in Italian.
5. Repeat steps 2–4 for Spanish.
Expected Result: Application UI fully translates to the selected language without errors or missing text.

Title: Verify application fails gracefully without internet
Preconditions: Application installed, device has active internet connection
Test Steps:
1. Ensure device has internet access and application is running.
2. Disable internet connection (e.g., via airplane mode or network settings).
3. Attempt to access a feature requiring external data (e.g., weather updates).
4. Observe application behavior (e.g., error message, offline mode, crash).
Expected Result: Application displays a user-friendly error message indicating no internet connection and does not crash.

Title: Verify application reconnects after internet restoration
Preconditions: Application installed, device previously had internet disabled
Test Steps:
1. Disable internet connection on the device.
2. Attempt to use a feature requiring external data (e.g., weather API).
3. Observe error message or offline state.
4. Re-enable internet connection.
5. Refresh the feature or wait for automatic reconnection.
Expected Result: Application reconnects to the internet and fetches data successfully without manual intervention.

Title: Verify Firebase database stores and retrieves user data
Preconditions: Firebase database configured, application installed, test user account created
Test Data: Test user (name: "TestUser", email: "test@example.com"), test greenhouse (name: "Greenhouse1", location: "TestLocation")
Test Steps:
1. Log in as the test user.
2. Create a new greenhouse entry with the provided test data.
3. Log out and log back in as the same user.
4. Verify the greenhouse data is displayed correctly.
5. Delete the greenhouse entry.
6. Log out and log back in; verify the deleted greenhouse no longer appears.
Expected Result: Data is stored, retrieved, and deleted successfully without corruption or loss.

Title: Verify Firebase database handles concurrent user access
Preconditions: Firebase database configured, two test user accounts created, application installed on two devices
Test Steps:
1. Log in as User1 on Device A and create a greenhouse named "SharedGreenhouse."
2. Log in as User2 on Device B and attempt to create a greenhouse with the same name.
3. Observe the behavior (e.g., error message, duplicate entry, or successful creation).
4. Verify User1’s data remains intact.
Expected Result: System prevents duplicate entries or handles conflicts gracefully (e.g., appends a suffix or shows an error).

Title: Measure application response time for weather data fetch
Preconditions: Device has active internet, OpenWeatherMap API key configured
Test Steps:
1. Open the weather feature in the application.
2. Enter "Rome" as the location.
3. Record the time taken from submission to display of weather data.
4. Repeat the test 5 times.
Expected Result: Average response time does not exceed 3 seconds (as per typical performance expectations for such APIs).

Title: Measure database query response time for user data
Preconditions: Firebase database populated with test data, application installed
Test Steps:
1. Log in as the test user.
2. Navigate to the greenhouse list screen.
3. Record the time taken to load all greenhouse data.
4. Repeat the test 5 times.
Expected Result: Average response time does not exceed 2 seconds for loading user data.

Title: Measure time for new user to learn basic application functions
Preconditions: Fresh installation of the application on a new device, no prior user experience
Test Data: New user with no prior exposure to the application
Test Steps:
1. Provide the test user with minimal instructions (e.g., "Use this app to manage your greenhouse").
2. Ask the user to complete the following tasks:
a. Log in/create an account.
b. Add a new greenhouse.
c. View weather data for a location.
3. Record the total time taken to complete all tasks.
4. Observe any difficulties or errors encountered.
Expected Result: User completes all tasks within 10 minutes (as per "Tempo di apprendimento dell’interfaccia grafica" metric).

Title: Verify application recovers from database communication crash
Preconditions: Application installed, Firebase database accessible
Test Steps:
1. Simulate a database communication crash (e.g., via network throttling or Firebase emulator misconfiguration).
2. Attempt to perform an action requiring database access (e.g., saving greenhouse data).
3. Observe application behavior (crash, freeze, error message, recovery).
4. If the app recovers, verify data integrity (e.g., no lost entries).
Expected Result: Application displays an error message and allows the user to retry without data loss or permanent crash.

Title: Verify application compatibility with multiple Android versions
Preconditions: Application APK available, Android emulators/physical devices with target versions configured
Test Steps:
1. Install the application on an Android 8.0 emulator/device.
2. Perform basic functions (e.g., login, add greenhouse).
3. Repeat steps 1–2 for Android 9.0, 10, and 11.
4. Note any compatibility issues (crashes, UI errors, missing features).
Expected Result: Application runs without critical errors on all specified Android versions (as per "Numero di versioni di Android compatibili" metric).

Title: First-time user registration and login
Preconditions: System is installed on the user's device
Test Steps:
1. Launch the application for the first time
2. Select the registration option
3. Enter valid username, email, and password
4. Confirm registration
5. Log in using the newly created credentials
Expected Result: User account is created in the database and user is redirected to the greenhouse management page

Title: User login with existing credentials
Preconditions: User is already registered in the database
Test Steps:

2. Select the login option
3. Enter registered email and password
4. Confirm login
Expected Result: User is redirected to the greenhouse management page

Title: Display current weather conditions

Expected Result: Current weather conditions are displayed to the user

Title: View greenhouse status
Preconditions: User is registered and successfully logged in

User has at least one greenhouse in their area

Test Steps: 1. Navigate to the greenhouse data section

Title: Load historical greenhouse data as graphs
Preconditions:
User is registered and logged in
User has at least one greenhouse

Expected Result: System loads and displays historical greenhouse data in graphical format via API

Title: Connect system to greenhouse hardware
Preconditions:
User is registered and logged in
User has at least one greenhouse in their area
Test Steps:
1. Ensure hardware is connected and operational
2. Navigate to the greenhouse management section
Expected Result: System establishes connection with greenhouse hardware and maintains operational status

Title: Detect and report hardware anomalies
Preconditions:
User is registered and logged in
User has at least one greenhouse connected to the system
Test Steps: 1. Trigger a hardware anomaly (e.g., sensor failure)
Expected Result: System detects the anomaly and communicates it to the user via sensor data

Title: Modify Personal Data as a Registered User
Preconditions: User account exists in the system; user has successfully logged in.
Test Data: Valid user credentials (username/password), updated personal data (e.g., name, email, phone number).
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the user’s restricted area (profile section).
2. Locate the option to modify personal data.
3. Enter updated personal data (e.g., new name, email, phone number).
4. Confirm the changes (e.g., click "Save" or "Update").
Expected Result: The system saves the updated personal data, and the user account reflects the changes. The database contains the modified user account data.

Title: Verify Database Update After Personal Data Modification
Preconditions: User account exists in the system; user has successfully logged in; personal data has been modified.
Test Steps:
1. Modify personal data (e.g., email) via the user’s restricted area.
2. Log out and log back in with the updated credentials (if applicable).
3. Query the database for the user’s record.
Expected Result: The database record matches the updated personal data entered by the user.
